description: 20-slide executive DALP deck on digital assets for the Estonia market

slides.title: DALP for Estonia's Digital Asset Market
slides.subtitle: How SettleMint's platform fits Estonia's MiCA-ready digital asset infrastructure | April 2026

slides.title: Estonia Market Context
slides.subtitle: Digital assets regulation and opportunity in the Baltic gateway

slides.title: Estonia: the EU gateway for digital asset issuers
slides.body:
Estonia was among the first EU member states to regulate crypto asset service providers, issuing FIU-based licenses from 2017 and building a digital-first infrastructure that includes e-Residency, national digital ID, and the X-Road data exchange layer.
MiCA now supersedes the old FIU framework. All CASPs operating in Estonia must obtain an FSA license by July 1, 2026 or cease operations. The Estonian Financial Supervision Authority is the designated competent authority under MiCA for all in-scope services.
Entities licensed in Estonia gain EU passporting rights, enabling them to offer services across all EU and EEA member states from a single regulatory base. This makes Estonia an attractive domicile for digital asset programs targeting the broader European market.
The fintech ecosystem is mature and digital-first. Operational and compliance infrastructure is aligned to EU standards, shortening the path from product design to licensed operations compared to many other EU jurisdictions.

slides.title: Four digital asset opportunities in Estonia
slides.item1:
Tokenized securities
Compliant bond and equity token issuance under MiCA and EU prospectus rules. ERC-3643 with OnchainID for investor eligibility and transfer control across EU jurisdictions.
slides.item2:
E-money tokens
EUR-denominated stablecoin infrastructure aligned to MiCA Title III. Reserve management, redemption rights, and FSA reporting built into the issuance platform from day one.
slides.item3:
Cross-border settlement
XVP settlement for EUR-denominated instruments across EEA counterparties. Atomic delivery-versus-payment with policy-governed transfer conditions and full audit trail.
slides.item4:
Fund administration
Digital fund units with NAV-linked lifecycle management. Subscription, redemption, and distribution events governed by OnchainID eligibility checks and SMART policy modules.

slides.title: Why DALP
slides.subtitle: Lifecycle control for regulated digital asset operations

slides.title: DALP is the operating layer, not just an issuance tool
slides.body:
DALP is SettleMint's Digital Asset Lifecycle Platform. It provides a single control plane for designing, issuing, managing, and servicing digital assets across the full lifecycle, from instrument definition to maturity or redemption.
For Estonia-based operators, DALP covers the requirements that MiCA compliance introduces: investor eligibility enforcement, transfer restrictions, approval workflows, audit evidence, and FSA reporting readiness. These are built into the platform, not added as custom code.
The platform uses ERC-3643 as the compliance token standard, SMART extensions for instrument-specific lifecycle logic, OnchainID for verified on-chain identity and claims, ISMARTCustodian for custody-level controls, and ISMARTCollateral for collateral management workflows. All execution is EVM-based.
Seven asset types are supported: bonds, deposits, stablecoins, equity, funds, real estate, and precious metals. A single operational model applies across all of them, so an issuer who starts with bonds can extend to funds or stablecoins without rebuilding governance and integration patterns.

slides.title: Six DALP building blocks for Estonian operators
slides.item1:
Asset templates
Start from proven ERC-3643 asset structures for bonds, stablecoins, deposits, equity, and funds. Reuse across programs without rebuilding compliance logic.
slides.item2:
Identity and claims
OnchainID provides verified on-chain identity. Claims from KYC, AML, and accreditation checks govern who can hold and transfer each instrument.
slides.item3:
Policy modules
SMART policy extensions enforce transfer restrictions, investor eligibility, country rules, and MiCA compliance conditions before any on-chain state change.
slides.item4:
Operational approvals
Maker-checker and RBAC workflows separate design authority from issuance authority. Sensitive lifecycle actions require multi-role sign-off.
slides.item5:
Lifecycle actions
Issuance, coupon payment, redemption, forced transfer, and freeze actions are governed platform operations, not ad hoc contract calls. All activity is logged.
slides.item6:
Integration readiness
APIs and event streams connect DALP to custody providers, KYC platforms, core banking systems, and FSA reporting pipelines without custom middleware.

slides.title: Three platform value propositions for Estonian issuers
slides.col1:
Compliance-First Architecture
ERC-3643 and OnchainID enforce investor eligibility, transfer restrictions, and jurisdiction rules at the protocol level. Compliance is not a wrapper: it is part of every on-chain action. MiCA requirements map directly to platform controls.
slides.col2:
Full Lifecycle Coverage
Design, issuance, servicing, settlement, and redemption live on one platform. Estonian issuers avoid fragmented tooling and manual handoffs between issuance systems, custody providers, and compliance teams. One record, one audit trail.
slides.col3:
Multi-Asset Flexibility
Seven asset types on one operational model. Start with a compliant EUR bond program and extend to e-money tokens, fund units, or real estate tokens without redesigning governance. EU passporting opens each new product to the full EEA market.

slides.title: Operating Model
slides.subtitle: How DALP maps EU and Estonian requirements into live execution

slides.title: Estonian and EU requirements mapped to DALP
slides.body: Each requirement below reflects a specific FSA licensing or MiCA CASP obligation. The DALP mechanism column shows how the platform addresses the requirement through configuration, not custom development.
table_data: FSA license requires MiCA-aligned CASP controls by July 2026
ERC-3643 policy modules and audit logging
MiCA and prospectus rules restrict who may hold each instrument
OnchainID verified claims and transfer guards
FSA expects clear separation of design, approval, and execution authority
Internal and FSA review requires complete activity records

REST APIs, webhooks, and event stream connectors

slides.title: DALP maps directly to MiCA compliance requirements
slides.body:
MiCA introduces specific obligations for CASPs that go beyond general KYC and AML. DALP addresses these at the platform level, not as configuration add-ons.
Transfer restrictions: ERC-3643 enforces transfer conditions on every token movement. Only wallets holding valid OnchainID claims that satisfy the instrument's policy module can receive a transfer. This satisfies MiCA requirements for controlled distribution of e-money tokens and asset-referenced tokens.
Investor eligibility: OnchainID claims carry verified KYC, AML, accreditation, and country attributes. SMART policy modules evaluate these claims before any issuance, transfer, or redemption event. Eligibility is checked on-chain, not in a separate off-chain process.
Reporting and audit: Every platform action generates a timestamped log entry. DALP's monitoring layer provides FSA-ready activity records covering issuance, transfer, servicing, and redemption events. Reporting pipelines connect directly to compliance and regulatory reporting systems via API.

slides.title: Identity and compliance are on-chain, not afterthoughts
slides.col1:
OnchainID and KYC for Estonian operators
OnchainID provides a reusable verified identity layer for all participants: issuers, investors, and intermediaries. Claims issued by accredited KYC, AML, and identity providers are stored on-chain and evaluated at transaction time. Estonian e-Residency and national digital ID attributes can be integrated as claim sources. Investor onboarding becomes a one-time verified claim event rather than a per-instrument manual check.
slides.col2:
Policy modules for EU jurisdiction rules
SMART policy extensions translate MiCA compliance obligations into executable on-chain logic. Country rules restrict distribution to approved EEA jurisdictions. Holding limits enforce MiCA thresholds for e-money tokens. Accreditation claims gate access to instruments restricted to professional investors. Policy modules are configurable per instrument and per jurisdiction, enabling EU passporting without rebuilding compliance logic for each target market.

slides.title: Use Cases
slides.subtitle: Where DALP fits strongest in Estonia

slides.title: Tokenized EUR-denominated bonds for EU capital markets
slides.body:
Estonian corporate and government issuers can use DALP to issue digital bonds directly accessible to EU investors under MiCA and EU prospectus regulation, without building custom smart contract infrastructure.
The bond lifecycle is fully covered: instrument design with term sheet metadata, investor eligibility gating via OnchainID claims, primary issuance with FSA-notifiable audit records, coupon payment processing, and maturity redemption. All on one platform.
ERC-3643 ensures every bond token has built-in transfer restrictions. Only verified investors in approved EEA jurisdictions can hold and trade the instrument. Forced transfer and freeze capabilities support regulatory intervention requirements.
EU passporting means an Estonia-licensed issuer can offer the same bond to investors across all EEA member states from a single regulatory filing. DALP's multi-jurisdiction policy modules handle country-level distribution rules without rebuilding the product for each market.

slides.title: E-money tokens and EUR stablecoin infrastructure
slides.body:
MiCA Title III introduces a clear licensing framework for e-money tokens. Estonia-licensed EMT issuers can use DALP to build compliant EUR stablecoin infrastructure with reserve management, redemption processing, and FSA reporting built into the platform.
Reserve management: DALP tracks the relationship between circulating token supply and reserve asset holdings. ISMARTCollateral manages collateral positions and ensures reserve coverage requirements are met and auditable at any point.
Compliance: Every token transfer enforces OnchainID eligibility checks. MiCA holding limits, country restrictions, and redemption rights are implemented as SMART policy modules. No custom contract development is required to satisfy FSA license conditions.
Instant settlement: EUR stablecoin transfers settle in seconds on the EVM layer. XVP settlement connects payment and delivery legs atomically for institutional use cases. Integration connectors link DALP to banking core systems and SEPA infrastructure for fiat on-ramp and off-ramp processing.

slides.title: Three more use cases with strong Estonian fit
slides.col1:
Real estate fractional ownership
Estonia's digital land registry and e-governance infrastructure supports tokenized real estate programs. DALP provides compliant fractional ownership tokens with investor eligibility controls, rental income distribution, and asset metadata management. ISMARTCollateral handles property-backed collateral positions.
slides.col2:
Fund administration with digital NAV
Digital fund units with NAV-linked lifecycle management. Subscription and redemption events are governed by OnchainID eligibility and SMART policy modules. Distribution calculations and investor reporting run on the same platform record. EU fund passporting applies to all registered EEA investors.
slides.col3:
Cross-border EUR settlement via XVP
Atomic delivery-versus-payment for EUR-denominated instruments across EEA counterparties. XVP settlement eliminates settlement risk between buyer and seller by linking asset delivery and payment in a single atomic transaction. Policy-governed transfer conditions ensure MiCA compliance on every settlement leg.

slides.title: Why SettleMint: platform depth, not consulting breadth
slides.body:
SettleMint delivers a platform. The engagement model is not advisory hours: it is a deployed, configurable system that Estonian operators own and operate.
Seven asset types are supported out of the box: bonds, deposits, stablecoins, equity, funds, real estate, and precious metals. Each asset type shares the same governance, identity, compliance, and servicing infrastructure. Issuers do not pay for re-implementation when they expand to a second or third product.
Deployment timelines are measured in weeks, not quarters. DALP is available as a self-hosted deployment on the operator's own infrastructure, a SettleMint-managed cloud instance, or a hybrid configuration. Operators choose based on their regulatory, data residency, and operational requirements.
The platform is EVM-native throughout. Smart contracts are audited, versioned, and upgradeable within the SMART extension framework. Estonian operators get full technical ownership of their deployed instance without dependency on SettleMint for ongoing contract management.

slides.title: Proposed next steps for Estonia
slides.body:
Pilot program: SettleMint can support a focused pilot covering one priority asset type, such as a EUR-denominated bond or an e-money token, within a defined scope and timeline. The pilot validates DALP's fit with FSA licensing requirements and establishes the operational baseline for a full rollout.
Technical workshop: A structured session with the Estonian operator's technology and compliance teams to map DALP's ERC-3643, OnchainID, and SMART policy architecture against the specific instrument types and MiCA obligations in scope. Output is a confirmed integration and compliance mapping document.
Compliance mapping session: A targeted review of the FSA license conditions and MiCA CASP obligations against DALP's built-in controls. Output is a gap analysis confirming which requirements are covered by platform configuration and which require operator-side process design.
To proceed, contact the SettleMint team to schedule the initial technical and compliance alignment session. Pilot scope and timeline can be agreed within two weeks of that session.
